message:
My friend Sarah will panic about *anything* — a smoke detector beeping at 2am? Instant meltdown. But last month when we were cooking together and a pan got a little too toasty on the stove, she actually stayed calm and turned off the heat, moved the pan, cracked a window. I was impressed.

Turns out when you know what to do, panic kind of... doesn't show up the same way. She'd actually talked through some basic kitchen safety stuff with her family a while back, and it just kicked in.

That's really all fire safety is, honestly — a few simple habits so your brain doesn't have to figure things out in the moment. Smoke alarms that work. A quick exit plan everyone actually knows. Not panicking in the kitchen. The unsexy stuff.

Does your house feel pretty solid on the basics, or is there one thing you've been meaning to tackle?
